questions.question: Explain the basic steps of the Insertion Sort algorithm and provide an example to illustrate its working on the array [5, 3, 8, 4, 2].
questions.hint: Describe the process of finding the 'gaps' and shifting elements accordingly

questions.question: Compare the time complexity of Insertion Sort with Bubble Sort. Why is Insertion Sort generally more efficient for partially sorted lists?
questions.hint: Discuss the impact of list order on algorithm efficiency

questions.question: Suppose we have an array [12, 3, 8, 1, 5] and we want to find the maximum element in this array using Insertion Sort. Describe the steps you would take and explain why it is not an efficient way to find the maximum element in the array.
questions.hint: Consider why you would not use Insertion Sort for this specific task
